# AU Corpus Scorecard Summary

This artifact moves the AU lane one step past the bounded handoff
checkpoint. It counts what is currently covered by persisted real
workbench bundles, and it keeps known raw transcript material visible
when it is not yet represented in that bundle set.

## Current real-bundle coverage

- Included real bundles: 4
- Workflow kinds: au_semantic, transcript_semantic
- Source labels: 4
- Statements: 13
- Observations: 40
- Facts: 13
- Events: 2
- Review queue items: 11
- Contested items: 3
- Approximate events: 2

## Bundle inventory

- wave1:real_au_procedural_v1 (au_semantic): 3 facts, 27 observations, 2 events, 3 review items.
- wave3:real_transcript_fragmented_support_v1 (transcript_semantic): 3 facts, 4 observations, 0 events, 2 review items.
- wave5:real_transcript_professional_handoff_v1 (transcript_semantic): 3 facts, 4 observations, 0 events, 2 review items.
- wave5:real_transcript_false_coherence_v1 (transcript_semantic): 4 facts, 5 observations, 0 events, 4 review items.

## Known raw-source backlog

- Raw transcript files currently present under `SensibLaw/demo/ingest/hca_case_s942025/media/transcripts`: 4
- File suffix breakdown: {".html": 1, ".json": 1, ".md": 1, ".txt": 1}

## Reading

- This is still a checkpoint, not a completeness proof over every AU
  source family we may ultimately care about.
- It is, however, materially stronger than reading the current 3-fact
  AU handoff slice as if it were the whole corpus.
